text: Also I heard the voice of the Lord, saying, Whom shall I send, and who will go for us? Then said I, Here am I; send me.

gcb.mirror: Calling begins with a vision of God's holiness.
gcb.water: Purification prepares the prophet for obedience.
gcb.fire: The sent one is tested by the word he carries.

text: Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.

gcb.mirror: God reveals deliverance through a sign beyond human power.
gcb.water: The promise sustains faith in national crisis.
gcb.fire: Messianic expectation burns through immediate history.

text: But he was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities.

gcb.mirror: Redemption is revealed through substitutionary suffering.
gcb.water: The servant bears what must be cleansed.
gcb.fire: Atonement passes through affliction and sacrifice.

text: Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee; and before thou camest forth out of the womb I sanctified thee.

gcb.mirror: Purpose in God precedes public mission.
gcb.fire: The prophet is tested by a destiny he did not choose.

text: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith the LORD, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.

gcb.mirror: Divine intention remains active even in exile.
gcb.water: Hope preserves a people under judgment.
gcb.fire: Promise is refined in long waiting.

text: Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that I will make a new covenant.

gcb.mirror: God reveals that renewal must reach the heart.
gcb.water: The covenant is rewritten inwardly, not merely externally.
gcb.fire: The new covenant burns through failed forms without denying divine faithfulness.

text: It is of the LORD'S mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not.

gcb.mirror: Even in ruin, mercy reveals what judgment has not destroyed.
gcb.fire: Hope survives because mercy outlasts wrath.

text: Be of good cheer, O my children, cry unto the Lord, and he will deliver you from the power and hand of the enemies.

gcb.water: Prayer keeps the community from dissolving in sorrow.
gcb.fire: Deliverance is expected through dependence, not denial.

text: A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you.

gcb.mirror: Restoration must reach the heart, not just the land.
gcb.water: God cleanses inwardly before outward rebuilding.
gcb.fire: The new heart burns away stony resistance.

text: Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and ye shall live.

gcb.water: Dry bones anticipate renewal and reordering.

text: And there was given him dominion, and glory, and a kingdom, that all people, nations, and languages, should serve him.

gcb.mirror: The true kingdom is given from above, not seized from below.
gcb.water: Vision clarifies history under divine rule.
gcb.fire: All earthly empires are judged by the everlasting kingdom.

text: And they that be wise shall shine as the brightness of the firmament.

gcb.mirror: Wisdom is revealed fully in the end.
gcb.water: Faithfulness prepares the soul for glory.
gcb.fire: Apocalyptic hope tests whether wisdom is lived now.
